zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Baker於1632年8月18日出生在英國英格蘭諾福爾可曼可洛福特時，她的父親John Baker Becker Sr.34歲，母親Hannah Beecher Langford48歲。她和James Watkins至少有1個女兒。她去世於1660年在美國麻薩諸塞州布里斯托斯萬希亞享年28歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭肯特希廷波恩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Baker于1632年8月18日出生在英国英格兰諾福爾可曼可洛福特时，她的父亲John Baker Becker Sr.34岁，母亲Hannah Beecher Langford48岁。她和James Watkins至少有1个女儿。她去世于1660年在美国马萨诸塞州布里斯托斯萬希亞享年28岁，埋葬在英国英格兰肯特希廷波恩。

en.New Haven, New Haven, Connecticut Colony: Newhaven Towne, New Haven, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America

nameMeanings.name: Baker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name, from Middle English bakere, Old English bæcere, a derivative of bacan ‘to bake’. It may have been used for someone whose special task in the kitchen of a great house or castle was the baking of bread, but since most humbler households did their own baking in the Middle Ages, it may also have referred to the owner of a communal oven used by the whole village. The right to be in charge of this and exact money or loaves in return for its use was in many parts of the country a hereditary feudal privilege. Compare Miller . Less often the surname may have been acquired by someone noted for baking particularly fine bread or by a baker of pottery or bricks.

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘baker’, for example Dutch Bakker , German Becker and Beck , French Boulanger and Bélanger (see Belanger ), Czech Pekař, Slovak Pekár, and Croatian Pekar .

History: Baker was established as an early immigrant surname in Puritan New England. Among others, two men called Remember Baker (father and son) lived at Woodbury, CT, in the early 17th century, and an Alexander Baker arrived in Boston, MA, in 1635.

variants.name: Miller
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a miller. The standard modern vocabulary word represents the northern Middle English term miller, an agent derivative of mille ‘mill’, reinforced by Old Norse mylnari (see Milner ). In southern, western, and central England Millward (literally, ‘mill keeper’) was the usual term. In North America, the surname Miller has absorbed many cognate surnames from other languages, for example German Müller (see Mueller ), Dutch Mulder and Molenaar , French Meunier , Italian Molinaro , Spanish Molinero , Hungarian Molnár (see Molnar ), Slovenian, Croatian, and Serbian Mlinar , Polish Młynarz or Młynarczyk (see Mlynarczyk ). Miller (including in the senses below) is the seventh most frequent surname in the US.

South German, Swiss German, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Müller ‘miller’ (see Mueller ) and, in North America, also an altered form of this. This form of the surname is also found in other European countries, notably in Poland, Denmark, France (mainly Alsace and Lorraine), and Czechia; compare 3 below.
Americanized form of Polish, Czech, Croatian, Serbian, and Slovenian   Miler   ‘miller’, a surname of German origin.

variants.name: Baxter
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English baxter ‘baker’ (from Old English bæcestre ‘baker’, earlier ‘female baker’, the feminine equivalent of bæcere). Compare Baker .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Baker was born on 18 August 1632, in Mancroft, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Baker Becker Sr., was 34 and her mother, Hannah Beecher Langford, was 48. She had at least 1 daughter with James Watkins. She died in 1660, in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 28, and was buried in Sittingbourne, Kent, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Baker est née le 18 août 1632, à Mancroft, Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Baker Becker Sr., avait 34 ans et sa mère, Hannah Beecher Langford, avait 48 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec James Watkins. Elle est décédée en 1660, à Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts, États-Unis, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterrée à Sittingbourne, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Baker am 18. August 1632 in Mancroft, Norfolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Baker Becker Sr. 34 und ihre Mutter Hannah Beecher Langford 48. Sie hatte mit James Watkins mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1660 im Alter von 28 Jahren in Swansea, Bristol, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten und wurde in Sittingbourne, Kent, England, United Kingdom begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Bakerが1632年8月18日にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクマンクロフトで生まれた時，父John Baker Becker Sr.は34歳，母Hannah Beecher Langfordは48歳であった。彼女はJames Watkinsとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1660年にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ブリストルスワンセアで28歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスケントシッティングボウルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Baker(이)가 1632년 8월 18일 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크 먼크로프트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Baker Becker Sr.(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hannah Beecher Langford(은)는 48세였다. 그녀는 James Watkins(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1660년 미국 매사추세츠 주 브리스톨 스완시에서 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 짓틴그보우른에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Baker родилась 18 августа 1632 г. в местности манкрофт, норфолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Baker Becker Sr. было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Hannah Beecher Langford, 48. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от James Watkins. Она умерла в 1660 г. в местности свансеа, бристол, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 28 лет и была похоронена в местности ситтингбурн, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания.
